title: Proposed new rates of excise duty on beer  - Canada.ca
content:
## Proposed new rates of excise duty on beer
On April 1, 2026 , the Government of Canada announced a proposed amendment to the Excise Act.
Based on this proposed amendment, the annual adjustment to the rates of excise duty on beer would remain capped at 2% for 2 years. Also, the rates of excise duty on the first 15,000 hectolitres of beer brewed in Canada would remain cut by half for 2 years.
This proposed amendment would only change the rates in effect as of April 1, 2026 , for the first 15,000 hectolitres of beer brewed in Canada. All other rates have already been adjusted by 2% .
Effective April 1, 2026 , you may report and remit excise duty on beer at the lower rates.
However, if you choose to pay the duty using the proposed new rates, you will be required to pay the difference in applicable duty if the legislation is not enacted by Parliament.
The proposed adjusted rates of excise duty on beer are:
Proposed rate effective April 1, 2026
Beer containing not more than 1.2% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume
$3.128 per hectolitre
Beer containing more than 1.2% but not more than 2.5% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume
$18.85 per hectolitre
Beer containing more than 2.5% absolute ethyl alcohol by volume
$37.69 per hectolitre
Percentage of absolute ethyl alcohol by volume in product
